title: Teenagers Arrested for Synagogue Attack in London
summary: In London, two teenagers were arrested by police for an attack on a synagogue. Someone threw a bottle with a flammable liquid through the window of the Kenton United Synagogue late at night, causing minor damage to the building. Luckily, no one was hurt. The police are investigating this as part of a series of attacks on Jewish places in London. A group called Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia has claimed responsibility for some of these attacks on social media. Police are checking if these claims are true. The deputy commissioner said these attacks are like a war fought by proxies and might involve people getting paid to do them. He praised the Jewish community for being strong. The Chief Rabbi called the attack cowardly and said it threatens everyone's safety and values.

background_read: This article is about a recent attack on a synagogue in London, which is a place where Jewish people gather to pray. Attacks like this are serious because they can hurt people and damage property. Police work to stop such crimes and keep communities safe. It's important to understand that everyone has the right to practice their religion without fear. The article mentions a group that might be behind these attacks, showing how social media can be used to spread messages about violence. Learning about different religions and respecting others helps build a peaceful society.
Article_Structure: WHO: Two teenagers and police officers
WHAT: An arson attack on a synagogue using a flammable bottle
WHERE: Kenton United Synagogue in north-west London
WHY: As part of a series of attacks on Jewish targets, possibly linked to a group and financial incentives

perspectives.description: The police see this as a serious crime and are investigating it as part of a larger pattern of attacks, possibly involving foreign influence and money.
The Jewish community feels threatened but shows resilience, with leaders condemning the violence as an attack on safety and shared values.
